text: We report the solar-driven production of hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) as a clean fuel through the photocatalytic reduction of dioxygen coupled with water oxidation in acidic aqueous media. Using [RuII(Me2phen)3]2+ as a visible-light-active photocatalyst and Ir(OH)₃ as a water oxidation catalyst, H₂O₂ was successfully synthesized in O₂-saturated sulfuric acid solutions. Mechanistically, photoinduced electron transfer from the excited state of the ruthenium complex to O₂ generates [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+ and a superoxide radical anion (O₂˙⁻). Under acidic conditions, O₂˙⁻ undergoes protonation and subsequent disproportionation to yield H₂O₂, a process that effectively competes with back electron transfer. The resulting ruthenium(III) species then oxidizes water back to O₂, facilitated by the Ir(OH)₃ nanoparticles. We further enhanced the efficiency of H₂O₂ production by substituting the iridium catalyst with the molecular complex [CoIII(Cp*)(bpy)(H₂O)]2+ in the presence of Sc(NO₃)₃. Under optimized conditions, the system achieved a quantum yield of 37% at 450 nm and a solar-to-chemical energy conversion efficiency of 0.25%.
